app.title: Cincinnati Civic Data Platform
app.subtitle: Public data for residents, advocates & journalists
app.attribution: Data from Cincinnati Open Data Portal
app.error: The city data API is temporarily unavailable.
app.noData: No data found for this query.

address.title: Address Lookup
address.description: Get a full public record for any Cincinnati address.
address.crimeDisclaimer: Addresses in crime data are approximate (last two digits replaced). Locations are skewed within the same block for privacy.
address.noPermits: No building permits found for this address.
address.noInspections: No inspections found for this address.
address.noAbatements: No tax abatements found for this address.
address.noCrime: No crime incidents found within 400 meters.
address.noBlight: No PLAP records found for this address.
address.noTransit: No SORTA bus stops found within 0.5 miles.

neighborhood.title: Neighborhood Profiles
neighborhood.description: A printable data profile for any Cincinnati neighborhood.
neighborhood.rentBurdenNote: % of households paying more than 30% of income on rent

police.title: Police Accountability
police.description: Search Cincinnati police transparency datasets in plain English.
police.query: Ask a question about the data
police.queryPlaceholder: e.g. How often were Black drivers searched in District 1 in 2023?
police.frozenNotice: This dataset is currently frozen pending CPD's transition to a new records management system.
police.rmsGap: Note: Crime data from the old RMS stops at 6/2/2024. A new dataset starts 6/3/2024. Both are combined for historical analysis.
police.disclaimer: This data is published by the City of Cincinnati for public accountability purposes. Patterns in the data do not establish intent or cause.

explorer.title: Neighborhood Explorer
explorer.description: Compare Cincinnati neighborhoods based on what matters to you.
explorer.allOff: Toggle dimensions below to start comparing neighborhoods.
explorer.noSelection: Enable at least one dimension to see results.
explorer.tractNote: Census tract boundaries do not perfectly align with neighborhood boundaries. Ambiguous assignments are flagged.

affordability.label: Affordability
affordability.description: Rent burden rate (% paying >30% of income on housing) and median gross rent.

income.label: Income Level
income.description: Median household income. You choose whether higher or lower income areas score better.

safety.label: Safety
safety.description: Crime incidents per 1,000 residents per year. Lower rate = higher score.

transit.label: Transit Access
transit.description: Number of SORTA bus stops within 0.4 miles of the neighborhood centroid.

investment.label: Neighborhood Investment
investment.description: Year-over-year change in building permit volume. Rising = improving trajectory.

blight.label: Public Maintenance
blight.description: PLAP blight activity per square mile and proportion of inspections passing first attempt.

parks.label: Park Access
parks.description: Total park acreage within 0.75 miles of the neighborhood centroid, per 1,000 residents. Source: CAGIS Parks and Greenspace.

flood.label: Flood Risk
flood.description: Whether the neighborhood centroid falls in a FEMA Special Flood Hazard Area (100-year zone). Source: FEMA National Flood Hazard Layer.

food.label: Food Access
food.description: % of neighborhood population living in a food desert tract (low income + supermarket >1 mile away). Source: USDA Food Access Research Atlas 2019.

schools.label: School Quality
schools.description: CPS school performance data is not available via public API. For school information, visit the Ohio Dept. of Education report card.

methodology.title: How neighborhoods are scored
methodology.body: Each enabled dimension produces a score from 0–100 for every neighborhood, normalized across the city (100 = best in class, 0 = worst). The final composite score is the weighted average of all enabled dimension scores, using your star weights. Gray neighborhoods have insufficient data on one or more enabled dimensions. Ties are broken alphabetically.

caveats.census: Census ACS data is survey-based with margins of error. Small neighborhoods may have high uncertainty.
caveats.tracts: Census tract boundaries do not align perfectly with Cincinnati's 52 neighborhoods. Where a tract spans multiple neighborhoods, data is attributed to the neighborhood containing the tract centroid.
caveats.crime: Crime data uses approximate locations (privacy-skewed to block level). Rates per neighborhood are directionally accurate but not precise.
caveats.schools: 'School quality' is explicitly absent from this tool. See the Ohio Dept. of Education report card at reportcard.education.ohio.gov.
